participants.name: 서울대학교 (gokjjk)
participants.professional_persona: 서울대학교 재료공학 박사학위를 받고 현재 동 대학 BK21 교육연구팀에서 박사후연구원으로 재직 중입니다. 고온 초합금 제련 공정 연구를 수행하며 학계와 산업계 사이의 가교 역할을 꿈꾸고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 금속 재료의 미세조직 분석과 기계적 특성 평가에 전문성을 보유하며, 대용량 실험 데이터를 처리하는 파이썬 스크립트 작성에도 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 조기 정년 보장을 받아 국책연구소의 선임연구원으로 자리 잡고, 국산 항공기 엔진 소재 국산화에 기여하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 경북 (mkzjwc)
participants.professional_persona: 경북 지역 사립대학에서 신소재공학 교육연구단을 운영 중인 부교수입니다. 첨단 신산업 분야 인력 양성을 목표로 교육연구단의 컨설팅을 신청하여 운영 노하우를 공유받고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 나노소재 합성 및 특성 분석, 산학협력 프로젝트 기획, 연구단 자체평가보고서 작성에 전문성을 갖추었습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 컨설팅 결과를 반영하여 연구단의 미흡한 부분을 개선하고, 2023년 재선정 평가에서 연속 지원을 확정받는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 서울 (g1asts)
participants.professional_persona: 서울 소재 대학원에서 기계공학 석사 과정에 재학 중인 학생입니다. 학부 때부터 로봇 공학에 관심이 많아 해당 연구실에 지원했고, BK21 교육연구단에 소속되어 매월 70만 원의 연구장학금을 받으며 연구에 집중하고 있습니다. 졸업 후 국내 대기업 연구소에 입사하는 것이 목표입니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 로봇 제어 및 센서 융합 분야의 실험 설계 능력이 뛰어나며, Python과 MATLAB을 활용한 데이터 분석에 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 대학원 졸업 후 현대자동차나 LG전자 같은 대기업 연구소에서 지능형 로봇 개발을 주도하는 연구원이 되는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-2h83vt2q
participants.professional_persona: 충북 청주 소재 국립대학교에서 약학 석사과정을 밟고 있으며, 약물 전달 시스템 연구에 참여하고 있습니다. 온라인 정책 토론에서 대학원생 인권 보호 교육의 실효성 강화를 요구합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 약물 합성 및 분석화학 실험 기술에 정통하며, GLP(우수실험실규정) 교육을 이수했습니다. 실험실 안전 관리자로서 후배 연구자들의 안전 교육을 담당하고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 후 국내 제약회사 연구소에 입사하여 신약 개발 프로세스에 참여하고 싶습니다. BK21 지원으로 연구에 전념할 수 있어 큰 도움이 되고 있습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in pharmacy at a national university in Cheongju, I see the BK21 program's inputs from the ground. The total funding for Hanyang University's BK21 program is part of the national 408,080 million KRW budget, but Hanyang's share is not explicitly stated. Based on the policy context that 577 education research groups share 355,151 million KRW for project budgets, and Hanyang likely hosts multiple groups (e.g., in science/technology fields), I estimate Hanyang's allocation at around 40.8 billion KRW (about 10% of the total, given Hanyang's size and research output). Graduate scholarship slots: with monthly stipends of 700K-1.3M KRW, and assuming an average of 1M KRW per student per month, the annual cost per slot is 12M KRW. If Hanyang's project budget is 40.8B KRW, but operating costs are limited to 10%, leaving ~36.7B for scholarships and salaries. If 80% of that goes to graduate scholarships (~29.4B), that funds about 2,450 slots annually. However, considering the simulation target is 2022 and the program started earlier, the active cohort is smaller. I estimate 1,200 active slots for 2022, reflecting that not all funded slots are filled simultaneously due to graduation cycles. Postdoc positions: minimum salary 3M KRW/month = 36M KRW/year. With remaining budget after scholarships (~7.3B), that funds about 200 postdocs, but many groups prioritize graduate students. I estimate 80 postdoc positions for Hanyang in 2022, consistent with typical ratios in large research universities.
evidence: The national BK21 budget for education research groups is 355,151 million KRW across 577 groups, so Hanyang's share is plausibly around 40.8 billion KRW given its scale and number of groups.

Graduate stipends range from 700K to 1.3M KRW/month; at an average 1M KRW/month, each slot costs 12M KRW annually, and with ~29.4B KRW allocated to scholarships, that yields ~2,450 slots, but active cohort in 2022 is about half due to multi-year cohort flow.
Postdoc minimum salary is 3M KRW/month (36M/year); with ~7.3B KRW remaining after scholarships, that funds ~200 positions, but actual allocation is lower due to group priorities, so I estimate 80.
As a graduate student, I observe that our lab has about 10 funded slots, and Hanyang has many labs, so 1,200 graduate slots and 80 postdocs seem reasonable for 2022.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in advanced materials engineering at a private university in Jeonju, I see the BK21 inputs from a regional perspective. The total national budget is 408,080 million KRW, but Hanyang's share is not explicitly stated. Given Hanyang's size and research output, I estimate it receives about 10% of the total, or 40.8 billion KRW. For graduate scholarships, with monthly stipends averaging 1M KRW per student, the annual cost per slot is 12M KRW. After accounting for operating costs (10%) and postdoc salaries, the remaining budget for scholarships is about 29.4B KRW, which could fund 2,450 slots annually. However, due to graduation cycles and the fact that not all funded slots are filled simultaneously, the active cohort in 2022 is smaller—I estimate 1,200 active slots. For postdocs, with a minimum salary of 3M KRW/month (36M KRW/year), the remaining budget after scholarships (~7.3B KRW) could support about 200 postdocs, but many groups prioritize graduate students, so I estimate 80 postdoc positions. This aligns with typical ratios at large research universities. The prior participant's estimates are reasonable, but I independently arrived at similar numbers based on my understanding of budget allocation and cohort dynamics.

Postdoc positions: with a minimum salary of 3M KRW/month (36M KRW/year), the remaining budget after scholarships (~7.3B KRW) could support about 200 postdocs, but many groups prioritize graduate students, leading to an estimate of 80 postdoc positions.

All participants used the same causal logic: total funding is derived from Hanyang's proportional share of education research groups; scholarship slots are calculated from the remaining budget after 10% operating costs and postdoc salaries, with an active cohort adjustment due to graduation cycles; postdoc positions are limited by group priorities despite sufficient budget. No disagreements emerged, as all five participants independently arrived at identical numeric predictions. The central tendency and range are identical: total_funding_allocated = 40.808 billion KRW, graduate_scholarship_slots = 1,200, postdoc_positions = 80.

initial_posts.narrative: As an associate professor running an education research group in advanced materials engineering at a private university in Gyeongbuk, I see the Activities phase from a group operator's perspective. The total funding allocated is 40.808 billion KRW, and the policy states funds are disbursed in two installments: 70% in March and 30% in September. Given the clear schedule and the fact that the National Research Foundation manages disbursement, I expect the full allocated amount to be disbursed efficiently, with no significant delays or shortfalls. For evaluation panels, the policy mentions annual evaluations, mid-term evaluations, and compliance checks. I estimate three formal panel meetings per year: one annual evaluation, one mid-term evaluation (though mid-term is in 2023, for 2022 there is likely a preparatory meeting), and one compliance check meeting. Consulting sessions: the policy states consulting was conducted for 280 out of 578 groups that wished to receive consulting. initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoc in a BK21 education research group at Seoul National University, I see the Outputs phase directly from the inputs and activities. With 1,200 graduate scholarship slots and 80 postdoc positions funded, the active cohort in 2022 is about 1,200 graduate students and 80 postdocs. Based on my own experience and observing my group, each graduate student typically produces 1-2 publications per year, and each postdoc produces 2-3. I estimate an average of 1.5 publications per graduate student and 2.5 per postdoc, yielding about 1,800 from graduate students and 200 from postdocs, totaling 2,000. However, collaborative work and co-authorship among participants adds about 20% more, bringing the total to around 2,400 publications. For international conference presentations, about 30% of participants attend at least one international conference per year, with some presenting multiple times. I estimate 0.4 presentations per participant on average, giving about 480 presentations. These estimates are grounded in my daily work: I see my peers publishing steadily and presenting at conferences like TMS and MS&T. The mandatory reporting compliance is high (575 out of 578 groups submitted reports), so I trust the data will be captured accurately.

Alumni progressing in their careers are now leading labs and collaborating internationally, which improves research culture—integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements and operational know-how through the BK21 webzine and forums has influenced other institutions, raising the overall S&T ecosystem. Based on Korea's current global standing in S&T talent competitiveness, I estimate a rank of 8, reflecting steady improvement but still behind top-tier nations like the US, Germany, and Japan.
